hypothesis: If Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) using a physics-based hierarchical reward derived from synthesis objectives improves the generation of BCC/B2 alloy candidates by achieving higher rates of phase coexistence (BCC and B2), ensuring BCC forms first and B2 exists at room temperature while maintaining <10% non-BCC/B2 phases, compared to baselines, then physics-informed preference learning with Thermo-Calc feedback enhances materials design with language models.
